issues.title: ZoomIt activates on AltGr+key instead of Ctrl+key
issues.next_action_reason: Community has supplied version, repro, and root cause plus images; team owes acknowledgement + routing. Confirm root cause, offer remap workaround, and request exact layout/keys to reproduce.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the detailed reports and screenshots. To confirm the root cause: on AZERTY/QWERTZ layouts Windows generates AltGr as Ctrl+Alt, so characters like `(`, `[`, `]`, `\` typed via AltGr collide with ZoomIt's Ctrl-based shortcuts (e.g. `Ctrl+(` = Record window), which is why ZoomIt intercepts them. As a workaround you can remap the conflicting ZoomIt shortcuts (Record window / Record region / Enter timer mode) to keys that don't clash with your layout's AltGr combos. We're tracking this as a real bug for non-US layouts and will look at making ZoomIt's default hotkeys layout-aware. Could you each confirm your exact Windows keyboard layout and the specific AltGr combination that's being hijacked so we can reproduce precisely?

issues.title: WSL 2 mirrored networking breaks NFS mounts ("Connection timed out")
issues.next_action_reason: Long-standing high-reach networking bug with community-verified root cause + workaround that the team has never acknowledged; ack the workaround, add labels, and prioritize a real fix.
issues.suggested_reply:
Thanks everyone for the detailed investigation here. Summarizing the community findings for the team and other users:

- Root cause appears to be that in mirrored networking mode, NFS mounts using privileged (reserved, <1024) source ports fail to complete the TCP handshake (SYN+ACK not delivered to the WSL socket).
- Confirmed workaround: mount with the `noresvport` option, e.g. `sudo mount -t nfs -o noresvport <HOST>:/share /mnt/share` (also works via `/etc/auto.net` / autofs and docker-compose).

We'll add `workaround-available` and keep this tracked for a proper fix. Could someone still hitting this on the latest 2.7.x confirm whether `noresvport` resolves it for them so we can prioritize?

issues.title: Cursor Misalignment and Missing Close Button (Dual Screens)
issues.next_action_reason: Frustrated community reporter (spotlight) with a corroborated dual-screen cursor-offset bug and no human reply in 28 days — acknowledge, label window-management, ask the DPI/monitor clarifying questions, and cluster with #935/#1233.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @k8redfern (and @leokyler for confirming) — sorry for the frustration here. To make sure we chase the right thing: this looks like a cursor-to-surface coordinate offset that appears on multi-monitor / mixed-DPI setups, which then makes the title-bar close button effectively unreachable. A couple of details that would help us reproduce: (1) are your two displays at different scaling factors (e.g. 100% + 150%) or different resolutions? (2) does the offset scale with the DPI of the monitor the window is on? (3) does it also happen with a single monitor? Logs are already attached (thank you). Labeling this window-management and adding it to our multi-monitor cursor-alignment investigation.

issues.title: [ARM64 / Qualcomm Adreno 680] GPU acceleration unavailable in WSL2 — /dev/dri/renderD128 not created
issues.next_action_reason: Confirm whether Adreno 680/685 supports WDDM GPU virtualization; if not, this is expected llvmpipe fallback.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks. As @-the-commenter noted, GPU-PV in WSL requires the Windows GPU driver to expose GPU virtualization (WDDM paravirtualization). If the Adreno 680 driver doesn't advertise that, `/dev/dri/renderD128` won't be created and Mesa correctly falls back to llvmpipe — this would be a Qualcomm driver limitation rather than a WSLg bug. Could a maintainer confirm the current ARM64 GPU-PV support matrix?

issues.title: WSLg PulseAudio connects successfully but audio streams time out (paplay) and microphone capture produces empty recordings
issues.next_action_reason: Well-isolated audio-stack bug with logs attached and no reply in ~10 days — acknowledge, label audio-redirection, and ask the three localizing questions (sink state, scope, weston.log).
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @EnZa666 — the isolation is helpful (control channel works: `pactl info` returns RDPSink/RDPSource, but every stream op — `paplay`, `parecord`, even `pactl suspend-sink` — times out). That pattern points at the WSLg PulseAudio RDP module's data/stream path (RDP audio-in/out virtual channel) rather than app config. A few things to help us localize it: (1) does `pactl list sinks short` / `pactl list sources short` show the RDP devices as RUNNING or SUSPENDED when you try to play? (2) does Windows-side audio playback from other WSLg apps work at all, or is all audio affected? (3) can you check `/mnt/wslg/weston.log` for RDP audio channel errors around the timeout? Labeling audio-redirection and adding it to the RDP-audio investigation. (Thanks for attaching the WslLogs bundle.)
